config_ton: tragic and sober, epic distance with intimate moments; violence without glorification; moral ambivalence toward empire and resistance

production_notes_color_audit.color_strategy_text: The mountain world begins in saturated earth tones — ochre, terracotta, firelight amber — and progressively desaturates as Hadji Murad moves deeper into Russian control. The imperial world (Tiflis, Petersburg) is always golden but cold: gilded surfaces that produce no warmth. The final sequence at the thicket ditch returns the palette to raw earth, with the single red of HM's blood echoing the opening thistle — suggesting not defeat but completion of an unbroken moral arc. Nicholas I's Petersbur
